currentTask.name: All 5 structural upgrades from 9Router comparative analysis implemented
currentTask.goal: Convert phase-gate.sh from hardcoded delegation to convention-based plugin discovery, add dynamic autonomy cascade, decision pipeline composition, cooldown + backoff in error-counter, and unified session dashboard — all inspired by 9Router's pipeline architecture.

whatChanged: Gate Plugin Discovery: phase-gate.sh quality checks section replaced hardcoded case statement with auto-discovery from scripts/gates/{phase}/*.sh. 8 plugin wrappers created. New scope-check gate added.
Dynamic Autonomy Cascade: autonomy-gate.sh start/adjust/status subcommands allow mid-phase autonomy level adjustment based on error-counter, comprehension-gate, and file-decision signals.
Decision Pipeline Composition: decision-pipeline.sh chains model-select → file-decision → autonomy-gate → comprehension-gate → quality-speed-gate at phase transitions.
Cooldown + Exponential Backoff: error-counter.sh now calculates cooldown = COOLDOWN_BASE × 2^(count-1) after each increment. Supports --retry-after override. Check/list show remaining cooldown.
Session Dashboard: session-dashboard.sh aggregates phase state, gate status, decision history, error counts, and debt levels into single view.
quality-gate.sh: added scripts/ as known internal path prefix to prevent false source-citation warnings for internal gate paths.
filesTouched: scripts/test-smoke.sh (+P12 gate plugin tests, +P13 cooldown tests, +P14 dashboard tests)
verification: phase-gate.sh discovers correct plugins per phase (research:1, plan:2, implement:4, verify:1)
autonomy-gate.sh start/adjust/status tested end-to-end with signal-based level transitions
decision-pipeline.sh chains 5 decision types and stops at first failure
error-counter.sh cooldown tested: 30s → 60s → 120s exponential backoff, --retry-after override, escalation at threshold
session-dashboard.sh aggregates all gate state correctly
All gate plugins return standard exit codes (0/1/2/3)
keyLearnings: 9Router's core architectural insight — separate pipeline from state machine — maps cleanly to agent governance: decision pipelines are pure transforms, phase state is the state machine, gate plugins are the composition layer.
Exit code 3 (SKIP) for gate plugins is critical: gates auto-detect missing evidence and gracefully degrade rather than blocking with confusing errors.
Dynamic autonomy is the behavioral counterpart of 9Router's mid-request fallback: re-evaluate autonomy after every signal, not once at task start.
The 5-upgrade plan demonstrates that good architecture comparison yields composable, testable improvements that fit an existing system's conventions.
residualRisk: All 5 upgrades are on main but rely on agent-mediated invocation (I must remember to call --check-quality, session-dashboard, etc.). No daemon mode. Error-counter cooldown is advisory (check shows it, but blocking requires manual respect of cooldown).
immediateNextSteps: Check all worktrees are cleaned up: git worktree list
Review 108 tests for any flaky or slow tests that could be optimized
No push needed unless remote tracking required
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
Question Gate is automatic: vague requests trigger the Clarification Protocol before implementation
Gate plugins: phase-gate.sh <phase> --check-quality auto-discovers plugins from scripts/gates/<phase>/*.sh
Autonomy cascade: autonomy-gate.sh start | adjust | status for dynamic mid-phase autonomy
Decision pipeline: decision-pipeline.sh <from->to> [--evidence <dir>] chains decisions at transitions
Error cooldown: error-counter.sh increment <op> [--retry-after N] sets exponential backoff cooldown
Dashboard: bash scripts/session-dashboard.sh [--json] for aggregated gate state
doNotRepeat: 2026-05-15: session-state.json was a bare template with 'test entry' data — never leave uncommitted session-state dirt at session end.
